section.title: Security Considerations
section.t: All of the security considerations to apply here. In addition, this document addresses how security mechanisms, such as Kerberos V5 GSS-API , can be applied to the loosely coupled model.

section.title: Loosely Coupled
section.t:
Under this coupling model, the principal used to authenticate the metadata file is different than that used to authenticate the data file. For the metadata server, the RPC credentials would be generated by the same source as the client. For RPC credentials to the data on the storage device, the metadata server would be responsible for their generation. Such "credentials" SHOULD be limited to just the data file be accessed. Using Kerberos V5 GSS-API , some possible approaches would be:
a dedicated/throwaway client principal name akin to the synthetic uid/gid schemes.
authorization data in the ticket.
an out-of-band scheme between the client and metadata server.

Depending on the implementation details, fencing would then be controlled either by expiring the credential or by modifying the synthetic uid or gid on the data file. I.e., if the credentials are at a finer granularity than the synthetic ids, it might be possible to also fence just one client from the file.
